name: superpowers
description: An agentic skills framework & software development methodology that works: planning, TDD, debugging, and collaboration workflows.

interface.shortDescription: Planning, TDD, debugging, and delivery workflows for coding agents
interface.longDescription: Use Superpowers to guide agent work through brainstorming, implementation planning, test-driven development, systematic debugging, parallel execution, code review, and finish-the-branch workflows.
defaultPrompt: I've got an idea for something I'd like to build.
Let's add a feature to this project.

skillPreviews.name: brainstorming
skillPreviews.description: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.

skillPreviews.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
skillPreviews.description: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies

skillPreviews.name: executing-plans
skillPreviews.description: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints

skillPreviews.name: finishing-a-development-branch
skillPreviews.description: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup

skillPreviews.name: receiving-code-review
skillPreviews.description: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation

skillPreviews.name: requesting-code-review
skillPreviews.description: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements

skillPreviews.name: subagent-driven-development
skillPreviews.description: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session

skillPreviews.name: systematic-debugging
skillPreviews.description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes

skillPreviews.name: test-driven-development
skillPreviews.description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code

skillPreviews.name: using-git-worktrees
skillPreviews.description: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - ensures an isolated workspace exists via native tools or git worktree fallback

skillPreviews.name: using-superpowers
skillPreviews.description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions

skillPreviews.name: verification-before-completion
skillPreviews.description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always

skillPreviews.name: writing-plans
skillPreviews.description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code

skillPreviews.name: writing-skills
skillPreviews.description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
